titles.text: A Phase II single-arm trial to investigate tepotinib in advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) non-small cell lung cancer with MET exon 14 (METex14) skipping alterations or MET amplification (VISION)
Tepotinib Phase II in NSCLC harboring MET alterations

organizations.name: EMD Serono Research & Development Institute, Inc
organizations.identifier: EMD Serono Research & Development Institute, Inc

roles.name: Sponsor (for all countries except the USA and Japa

code.decode: Inconsistency And/or Error In The Protocol

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Informed Consent
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Signed, written informed consent by subject or legal representative prior to any trial-specific screening procedure;

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Age and Sex
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Male or female, ≥ 18 years of age (or having reached the age of majority according to local laws and regulations, if the age of majority is > 18 years of age [i.e., ≥20 years of age in Japan]);

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Measurable Disease
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Measurable disease by IRC in accordance with RECIST Version 1.1;

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: ECOG Performance Status
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Performance Status (ECOG PS) of 0 or 1;

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Female Contraception Requirements
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: A female subject is eligible to participate if she is not pregnant, not breastfeeding, and at least 1 of the following conditions applies: a. Not a woman of childbearing potential as defined in Appendix VIII OR b. A woman of childbearing potential who agrees to use a highly effective contraception (i.e., methods with a failure rate of less than 1% per year) as detailed in in Appendix VIII of this protocol 2 weeks before start of first dose of study treatment, during the treatment period and for at least 4 weeks after the last dose of study treatment. Women of childbearing potential must have a negative pregnancy test (β-HCG test in serum) prior to enrollment.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: NSCLC Diagnosis
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Histologically or cytologically confirmed advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) NSCLC (all types including squamous and sarcomatoid);

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Prior Therapy Lines
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Treatment naïve patients in first-line or pretreated patients with no more than 2 lines of prior therapy;

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: MET Alterations
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Subjects with MET alterations, namely: METex14 skipping alterations in plasma and/or tissue, as determined by the central laboratory or by an assay with appropriate regulatory status will, be enrolled into the trial. For these subjects, sufficient tumor tissue and/or plasma is requested to allow additional testing; MET amplification only in plasma defined by a positive LBx test, as determined by the central laboratory or by an assay with appropriate regulatory status; Based on the outcome of the interim analysis in 12 LBx selected subjects: MET amplification only in tissue defined by a positive TBx with a gain of at least 4 copies of the MET gene, as determined by the central laboratory or by an assay with appropriate regulatory status.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Symptomatic Brain Metastases
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Subjects with symptomatic brain metastases who are neurologically unstable, and/or have required an increase in steroid dose within 2 weeks and/or have received prior stereotactic radiosurgery/gamma knife within 2 weeks and/or other prior treatment for brain metastases within 4 weeks prior to the start of therapy. Subjects with leptomeningeal disease are ineligible;

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Unresolved Toxicity
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Any unresolved toxicity Grade 2 or more according to National Cancer Institute-Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (NCI-CTCAE) Version 4.03, from previous anticancer therapy;

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Need for Transfusion
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Need for transfusion within 14 days prior to the first dose of trial treatment;

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Brain Metastasis as Only Measurable Lesion
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Subjects who have brain metastasis as the only measurable lesion.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: EGFR Activating Mutations
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Subjects with characterized EGFR activating mutations that predict sensitivity to anti-EGFR therapy;

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: ALK Rearrangements
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Subjects with characterized ALK rearrangements that predict sensitivity to anti-ALK therapy;

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Prior Anticancer Therapy
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Prior chemotherapy, biological therapy, radiation therapy, hormonal therapy for anti-cancer purposes, targeted therapy, or other investigational anticancer therapy (not including palliative radiotherapy at focal sites) within 21 days prior to the first dose of trial treatment.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Inadequate Hematological Function
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Inadequate hematological function: Hemoglobin < 8.5 g/dL; Neutrophils < 1.5 × 109/L; Platelets < 100 × 109/L.

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Inadequate Liver Function
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Inadequate liver function: total bilirubin > 1.5 × upper limit of normal (ULN); AST/ALT > 3 × ULN; For subjects with liver metastases: total bilirubin > 1.5 × ULN, AST/ALT > 5 × ULN

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Inadequate Renal Function
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Inadequate renal function: Severe renal impairment as evidenced by: Serum creatinine ≥1.5 × ULN; Creatinine clearance (CrCl) < 30 mL/min calculated by the Cockcroft-Gault formula (24 hour CrCl might be requested by the Investigator for confirmation, if calculated CrCl is < 30 mL/min. In such case, subjects with 24 hour CrCl < 30 mL/min should be excluded) CrCl (mL/min) = [140 – age (year) × weight (kg)] {× 0.85 for female subjects} / 72 × serum creatinine (mg/dL)

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Prior HGF/c-Met Pathway Agents
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Prior treatment with other agents targeting the HGF/c-Met pathway

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Impaired Cardiac Function
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Impaired cardiac function: a. Left ventricular ejection fraction < 45% defined by echocardiography (a screening assessment not required for subjects without a history of congestive heart failure unless clinically indicated); b. Serious arrhythmia; c. Unstable angina pectoris; d. New York Heart Association heart failure class III and IV; e. Myocardial infarction within the last 12 months prior to trial entry; f. Symptomatic pericardial effusion

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Uncontrolled Hypertension
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Hypertension uncontrolled by standard therapies (not stabilized to < 150/90 mmHg)

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: History of Other Neoplasm
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Past or current history of neoplasm other than NSCLC, except for curatively treated non-melanoma skin cancer, in situ carcinoma of the cervix, or other cancer curatively treated and with no evidence of disease for at least 5 years

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Gastrointestinal Conditions
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Medical history of difficulty swallowing, malabsorption, or other chronic gastrointestinal disease, or conditions that may hamper compliance and/or absorption of the test product

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Major Surgery
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Major surgery within 28 days prior to Day 1 of trial treatment

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Infections
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Known infection with human immunodeficiency virus, or an active infection with hepatitis B or hepatitis C virus

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Other Medical/Psychiatric Conditions
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Substance abuse, active infection, or other acute or chronic medical or psychiatric condition or laboratory abnormalities that might increase the risk associated with trial participation at the discretion of Investigators

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Hypersensitivity
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Known hypersensitivity to any of the trial treatment ingredients

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Legal Incapacity
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Legal incapacity or limited legal capacity

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Investigator Discretion
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Any other reason that, in the opinion of the Principal Investigator, precludes the subject from participating in the trial

eligibilityCriterionItems.name: Participation in Other Clinical Trial
eligibilityCriterionItems.text: Participation in another clinical trial within the past 30 days.

arms.name: Tepotinib Monotherapy
arms.description: Subjects receive tepotinib monotherapy at 500 mg once daily for advanced NSCLC with METex14 skipping alterations or MET amplification.

objectives.name: Primary Efficacy Objective for Part 1: Cohort A
objectives.text: To assess the efficacy of tepotinib in subjects with advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), as per objective response (confirmed complete response [CR] or partial response [PR]) determined according to Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors (RECIST) Version 1.1, based on independent review in: Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations, regardless of MET amplification status; Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations based on liquid biopsy (LBx), regardless of MET amplification status; Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations based on tumor biopsy (TBx), regardless of MET amplification status.

endpoints.name: Primary Objective Response Rate
endpoints.text: Objective response (confirmed CR or PR) determined according to RECIST Version 1.1, based on independent review (IRC).

objectives.name: Secondary Efficacy Objective
objectives.text: To further assess the efficacy of tepotinib in terms of: Objective response as per Investigator; Duration of response by Independent Review Committee (IRC); Duration of response by Investigator; Objective disease control as per IRC; Objective disease control as per Investigator; Progression free survival as per IRC; Progression free survival as per Investigator; Overall survival.

endpoints.name: Objective Response Rate by Investigator
endpoints.text: Objective response (confirmed CR or PR) determined according to RECIST Version 1.1, as per Investigator

endpoints.name: Progression Free Survival by IRC
endpoints.text: Progression free survival (PFS) as per IRC

endpoints.name: EORTC QLQ-C
endpoints.text: EORTC QLQ-C30

endpoints.name: Number of Subjects with Markedly Abnormal Clinical Laboratory Tests
endpoints.text: Number of subjects with markedly abnormal clinical laboratory tests

objectives.name: Secondary Safety and Tolerability Objective
objectives.text: To assess the tolerability and safety of tepotinib in terms of: Number of subjects with treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs) and deaths; Number of subjects with markedly abnormal clinical laboratory tests; Number of subjects with markedly abnormal vital signs, electrocardiogram (ECG), and physical examination.

endpoints.name: Duration of Response by IRC
endpoints.text: Duration of response as per IRC

endpoints.name: Progression Free Survival by Investigator
endpoints.text: Progression free survival as per Investigator

endpoints.name: QLQ-LC
endpoints.text: QLQ-LC13.

endpoints.name: Number of Subjects with Markedly Abnormal Vital Signs, ECG, Physical Examination
endpoints.text: Number of subjects with markedly abnormal vital signs, ECG, physical examination, including change in body weight and Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group Performance Status.

objectives.name: Secondary Pharmacokinetic Objective
objectives.text: To assess pharmacokinetics (PK) of tepotinib and its metabolite(s)

endpoints.name: Duration of Response by Investigator
endpoints.text: Duration of response as per Investigator

endpoints.name: Overall Survival
endpoints.text: Overall survival (OS).

endpoints.name: Number of Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events and Deaths
endpoints.text: Number of TEAEs based upon the Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities and Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events of the National Cancer Institute Version 4.03

endpoints.name: Plasma Concentrations for Population PK Analysis
endpoints.text: Plasma concentrations from sparse PK sampling to allow population PK analysis. Sparse PK sampling will be performed predose, at 1.5 hours postdose and at 4 hours postdose on Cycle 1, Day 1 and on Cycle 2, Day 1. Endpoints include, but are not limited to, CL/f and V/f derived from plasma concentrations. Population PK analysis will be reported separately. The PK of tepotinib and its metabolites will be assessed using a population PK analysis approach.

objectives.name: Secondary Health-Related Quality of Life Objective
objectives.text: To assess Health-Related Quality of Life.

endpoints.name: Objective Disease Control Rate by IRC
endpoints.text: Objective disease control (DCR) as per IRC

endpoints.name: EQ-5D-5L
endpoints.text: EQ-5D-5L

endpoints.name: Number of Deaths
endpoints.text: Number of deaths

objectives.name: Exploratory Biomarker Objective
objectives.text: To explore a possible link between biomarkers of mesenchymal-epithelial transition factor (c-Met) pathway activation, other relevant oncogenic pathways in plasma, serum and tumor tissue, and the activity of tepotinib

endpoints.name: Biomarkers of c-Met Pathway Activation and Other Oncogenic Pathways
endpoints.text: Biomarkers of c-Met pathway activation and other relevant oncogenic pathways in plasma, serum and tumor tissue and their potential correlation with the activity of tepotinib.

objectives.name: Exploratory QT/QTc Interval Objective
objectives.text: To explore the QT/QTc interval concentration relationship based on Cycle 1, Day 1 and Cycle 2, Day 1 data

endpoints.name: QT/QTc Interval Concentration Relationship
endpoints.text: QT/QTc interval concentration relationship based on Cycle 1, Day 1 and Cycle 2, Day 1 data

endpoints.name: Exposure-Response Relationship
endpoints.text: Exposure-response relationship

objectives.name: Exploratory Efficacy Objective for Part 1: Cohort B Subgroups
objectives.text: To assess efficacy of tepotinib in subjects with advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) NSCLC, in: Subjects with gene copy number (GCN) gain ≥ 4 and < 6 irrespective of LBx test result; Subjects with GCN gain ≥ 6 irrespective of LBx test result.

endpoints.name: Objective Response in GCN Gain Subgroups
endpoints.text: Objective response (confirmed CR or PR) in subjects with gene copy number (GCN) gain ≥ 4 and < 6 irrespective of LBx test result; and in subjects with GCN gain ≥ 6 irrespective of LBx test result.

estimands.name: Primary Efficacy Estimand for Part 1: Cohort A (TBx or LBx Analysis Se
estimands.populationSummary: Adult patients with advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) non-small cell lung cancer harboring MET exon 14 (METex14) skipping alterations or MET amplification, meeting eligibility criteria. (Subjects tested positive for METex14 skipping alterations irrespective of testing methodology (tumor biopsy or liquid biopsy) in Part 1: Cohort A.) Summary measure: Difference in proportions.

intercurrentEvents.name: Treatment discontinuation
intercurrentEvents.text: Subject discontinues study treatment (due to adverse event, disease progression, or withdrawal of consent) before achieving confirmed objective response.

intercurrentEvents.name: Death prior to response assessment
intercurrentEvents.text: Subject dies prior to objective response assessment.

population.name: Study Population
population.description: Target population defined by eligibility criteria

notes.text: Subject eligibility evaluation (review of inclusion/exclusion criteria) will be checked. Subjects may have a prescreening more than 28 days prior to first administration of trial treatment.
All visits and assessments from Cycle 2 onwards may be performed 3 days to accommodate unforeseen delays, holidays, or vacations.

Performed within 14 days of the last dose of trial treatment, reason for treatment discontinuation should be recorded. If the subject discontinues from the trial at a scheduled visit, the End of Treatment assessment can be performed on that day.

30-Day Safety Follow-up visit, performed at 30 ± 3 days after the last trial treatment for all subjects who discontinue trial treatment permanently, including subjects who have completed an End of Treatment visit.

Subjects who withdraw from the treatment for reasons other than PD have additional visits for tumor assessments. A ± 7 day time window is permitted for additional follow-up visits until 9 months, then ± 14 days thereafter. Reasons for study termination should be recorded if this visit is the last visit for the subject. Recording of any new anticancer therapy will be made (a tumor assessment is mandatory before initiating the new therapy).

Survival follow-up, to be performed every 3 months 2 weeks at clinic visit or by telephone contact. Subjects’ survival information will be collected. Any new anticancer therapy given to the subject until death should be recorded.
For subjects who have a prescreening ICF, this information will be recorded in prescreening.
Full medical/disease history will be recorded at Screening/Baseline.
Provision of a fresh or archived pretreatment tumor biopsy (excluding fine needle aspiration); please refer to the laboratory manual for details.
Optional tumor biopsy. Subjects need to sign a separate consent.
Only for women of childbearing potential, including those who have had a tubal ligation.

Assessments can be repeated at the Investigator’s discretion at unscheduled visits. To assess the safety and tolerability of the IMP. Serum pregnancy test is permitted if urine pregnancy test is not available at the site.
Not necessary if thoracic CT is performed as part of the screening RECIST Version 1.1 tumor assessment.
Only if last assessments were performed 7 days prior to End of Treatment visit.

Vital signs (pulse rate, systolic and diastolic blood pressure, body temperature, respiratory rate) are to be taken after at least 5 minutes rest in seated position. If vital signs and ECGs are taken at the same time, vital signs should be taken prior to each ECG recording.

On Cycle 1, Day 1 and on Cycle 2, Day 1, an ECG will be recorded predose (within 60 minutes prior to dose) and at 4 hours ± 12 minutes postdose. ECG recordings must be performed before PK sampling time points on days where both assessments are performed. The ECGs must be taken as triplicate 12-lead resting ECGs within 2 minutes after minimum of 10 minutes rest in supine position. Start of resting time, ECG and PK sampling time must be recorded in the electronic Case Report Form.

Single ECG predose only on Cycle 3, Day 1 (within 30 minutes prior to dose), after 5 minutes rest in supine position. After Cycle 3, single ECGs will be performed after 5 minutes rest in supine position every third cycle through Cycle 15 (i.e., Cycle 3, 6, 9, 12, and 15) or as clinically indicated. Beginning at Cycle 15, single ECGs will be performed after 5 minutes rest in supine position every odd cycle thereafter (Cycles 15, 17, 19, 21, etc) or as clinically indicated.
Single ECGs will be performed after 5 minutes rest in supine position.

Hemoglobin, hematocrit, red blood cell count, white blood cell count, differential white blood cells, platelets, and coagulation (prothrombin time, activated thromboplastin time, and International normalized ratio).
Laboratory tests for screening should be within 7 days prior to Cycle 1 Day 1. Laboratory assessments will be performed by the local laboratory.

Blood urea nitrogen, urea, creatinine, alanine aminotransferase, aspartate aminotransferase, gamma-glutamyl transpeptidase, total bilirubin (including direct fraction if total bilirubin abnormal), lipase, total amylase, total protein, albumin, alkaline phosphatase, creatinine clearance, sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium, glucose, and serum cystatin (for sites where the test is available).

Complete tumor assessment of all lesions by radiographic modality (using RECIST Version 1.1). CT or MRI of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis to evaluate disease in these locations. At Screening/Baseline, brain imaging by MRI with IV contrast enhancement; MRI may be performed without contrast enhancement, if contrast is contraindicated. If MRI is not clinically feasible, CT of the brain with IV contrast enhancement may be used. For subjects with brain metastases detected at Screening/Baseline only, subsequent brain imaging by MRI with IV contrast enhancement; MRI may be performed without contrast enhancement, if contrast is contraindicated. If MRI is not clinically feasible, CT of the brain with IV contrast enhancement may be performed; CT may be performed without contrast enhancement, if contrast is contraindicated. Additional anatomic areas should be investigated in case of suspicion of presence of metastases based on signs and symptoms of individual subjects.

Subjects will be assessed every 6 weeks following the Cycle 1, Day 1 visit until 9 months and every 12 weeks thereafter according to RECIST Version 1.1 until disease progression, death or withdrawal of consent (Investigator and independent read).
Only if last tumor assessment was performed ≥ 6 weeks within the first 9 months, or ≥ 12 weeks after 9 months prior to End of Treatment visit.

Sparse PK sampling (total of 6 samples) will be performed predose, at 1.5 hours postdose and at 4 hours postdose on Cycle 1, Day 1 and on Cycle 2, Day 1. The actual time of PK sample collection must be recorded. On days when PK samples are to be drawn, subjects should be instructed to attend the trial visit in a fasted state, with no breakfast and prior to taking their dose of tepotinib.
Predose only; collection will only be performed after signed consent for PGx testing has been obtained and only if local regulations allow.
On-treatment ctDNA samples to be taken at predose at Cycle 3, Cycle 5 and EoT only.

PROs (EQ-5D-5L, EORTC QLQ-C30 and QLQ-LC13): the questionnaires will be completed every 6 weeks from Cycle 1, Day 1 until 9 months and every 12 weeks thereafter until disease progression, death or withdrawal of consent. At all visits indicated including EoT and Safety Follow-up, every effort should be made to have the questionnaires completed by the subject prior to the initiation of any other trial activities (including RECIST Version 1.1 assessments) or active treatment and prior to any contact with the Investigator.

Consider provision of a FFPE tumor sample (a fresh or archived pretreatment tumor biopsy [excluding fine needle aspiration]) in case the subject is enrolled based on assays with appropriate regulatory status. Please refer to the respective laboratory manual for details.
To be taken at predose. Blood sample collection for exploratory biomarker research only if local regulations allow.

The blood sample is to be taken at screening if the subject is enrolled based on assays with appropriate regulatory status and no blood sample was provided during prescreening for determination of MET status in plasma.
To be performed only for subjects with a history of congestive heart failure or if clinically indicated.

studyInterventions.name: Tepotinib
studyInterventions.description: Investigational product for advanced (locally advanced or metastatic) non-small cell lung cancer with MET exon 14 (METex14) skipping alterations or MET amplification.

studyInterventions.name: Prior Chemotherapy
studyInterventions.description: Prior chemotherapy for anti-cancer purposes, requiring a 21-day washout period before the first dose of trial treatment.

studyInterventions.name: Prior Biological Therapy
studyInterventions.description: Prior biological therapy for anti-cancer purposes, requiring a 21-day washout period before the first dose of trial treatment.

studyInterventions.name: Prior Radiation Therapy
studyInterventions.description: Prior radiation therapy for anti-cancer purposes (excluding palliative radiotherapy at focal sites), requiring a 21-day washout period before the first dose of trial treatment.

studyInterventions.name: Prior Hormonal Therapy
studyInterventions.description: Prior hormonal therapy for anti-cancer purposes, requiring a 21-day washout period before the first dose of trial treatment.

studyInterventions.name: Prior Targeted Therapy
studyInterventions.description: Prior targeted therapy for anti-cancer purposes, requiring a 21-day washout period before the first dose of trial treatment.

studyInterventions.name: Other Investigational Anticancer Therapy
studyInterventions.description: Other investigational anticancer therapy, requiring a 21-day washout period before the first dose of trial treatment.

studyInterventions.name: Any other cancer therapy
studyInterventions.description: Prohibited concomitant therapy including chemotherapy, biological therapy, hormonal therapy for anticancer purposes, targeted therapy, or any investigational product other than tepotinib.

studyInterventions.name: P-gp, BCRP, OCT1, OCT2, MATE1, MATE2 inhibiting drugs
studyInterventions.description: Prohibited concomitant drugs that inhibit P-gp, BCRP, OCT1, OCT2, MATE1, and MATE2.

studyInterventions.name: P-gp inducing drugs
studyInterventions.description: Prohibited concomitant drugs known to induce P-gp (e.g., avasimibe, carbamazepine, phenytoin, rifampin, Saint John’s wort).

studyInterventions.name: P-gp transported drugs
studyInterventions.description: Permitted concomitant medications known to be transported by P-gp (e.g., rivaroxaban, apixaban, ranolazine, talinolol, digoxin), to be used with caution and close safety monitoring if they have a narrow therapeutic range.

studyInterventions.name: OCT2, MATE1, MATE2 transported drugs
studyInterventions.description: Permitted concomitant medications known to be transported by OCT2, MATE1, and MATE2 (e.g., dofetilide, metformin), to be used with caution and close safety monitoring if they have a narrow therapeutic range.

studyInterventions.name: OCT1 transported drugs
studyInterventions.description: Permitted concomitant medications known to be transported by OCT1, to be used with caution and close safety monitoring if they have a narrow therapeutic range.

studyInterventions.name: Highly effective contraception
studyInterventions.description: Required for male and female subjects of childbearing potential during and for a specified period after the treatment period.

studyInterventions.name: Supportive treatment
studyInterventions.description: Permitted supportive treatment (e.g., bisphosphonates, agents for improving appetite) if initiated prior to trial entry. Prophylactic bisphosphonates during trial should be avoided.

studyInterventions.name: Anticonvulsants
studyInterventions.description: Permitted anticonvulsants (lamotrigine, levetiracetam, pregabalin, valproic acid) for symptomatic treatment of brain metastasis with reduced risk for drug interactions.
